title: Joojeh Kabab (Saffron Chicken Kebab)
description: Juicy, tender chunks of chicken marinated in a vibrant blend of saffron, lemon, yogurt, and onions, grilled over an open flame.

ingredients.note: Ground, bloomed in 2 tbsp hot water

instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the grated onion, lemon juice, yogurt, olive oil, bloomed saffron, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to create the marinade.
Add the chicken chunks to the marinade. Toss thoroughly to ensure every piece is coated in the vibrant yellow mixture. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap.
Marinate the chicken in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours, ideally overnight, to allow the flavors to penetrate and the yogurt/lemon to tenderize the meat.
If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes. Thread the marinated chicken pieces onto the skewers, packing them slightly snug but not squished.
Preheat a grill to medium-high heat. If you don't have a grill, use a grill pan or the oven broiler.
Grill the chicken skewers for 10-15 minutes, turning every few minutes to ensure even cooking and light char marks.
During the last 2 minutes of grilling, brush the chicken pieces with melted butter mixed with a tiny drop of leftover bloomed saffron for extra richness and shine.
Remove from the grill and serve immediately.

tips: Grating the onion rather than slicing it releases more juice, which acts as a powerful tenderizer. Don't skip the saffron; it is the soul of this dish.
storage_notes: Store leftover cooked chicken in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days. Reheat gently in a pan or microwave. The marinade can be frozen with the raw chicken for up to 2 months.
serve_with: Saffron rice, grilled tomatoes, fresh flatbread (Lavash or Sangak), and raw onions.

substitutions.note: Some modern Persian cooks use a spoon of mayo instead of yogurt for an extra tenderizing fat coating.
